title: Who is Christian M'billi? The unbeaten puncher across from Canelo Álvarez
summary: Canelo Álvarez is one of the most recognisable names in boxing, but the more intriguing question in this super-middleweight title bout is the man opposite him. Here is what the numbers say about Christian M'billi.
body:
When a fight involves Canelo Álvarez, the famous name takes care of itself. The reason to look closer at this super-middleweight title bout, reported to have moved to late October, is the man in the other corner. Christian M'billi arrives unbeaten, younger and barely known to the wider audience, and the numbers make him worth understanding.

Christian M'billi, the unbeaten challenger

M'billi's record stands at 29-0-1. What stands out is how he gets there. Of his 29 wins, 24 have come by knockout, and only five have gone to the judges' scorecards. This is a fighter built around finishing rather than out-pointing opponents. The single mark against him is a draw. He has yet to be beaten as a professional.

At 31 he is four years the younger man, and his 72-inch reach makes him the longer fighter in the ring even though the pair stand the same 5ft 8in. His recent outings fit the punching profile. Across his last five he has mixed stoppages with decisions, two of those wins ending early. For a relative unknown stepping up to this level, a career spent ending fights is the headline an audience needs.

Canelo Álvarez, the established name

Álvarez is the known quantity. At 35 his 63-3-2 record spans 68 professional contests, with 39 wins inside the distance and 24 on the cards. He is as comfortable banking rounds as forcing a stoppage. Recent form has leaned on his judgement, a run of decision wins that suggests a boxer increasingly content to control fights rather than chase the knockout.

How they match up

  • Experience: Álvarez has been 68 professional contests deep, M'billi 30. That is the widest gap on the page.
  • Finishing: M'billi stops opponents far more often, roughly 83 per cent of his wins by knockout against Álvarez's 62.
  • Going the distance: Álvarez has 24 decision wins to M'billi's five, the clearer sign of a fighter proven over twelve hard rounds.
  • Reach: M'billi's 72 inches to Álvarez's 70 gives the challenger a two-inch edge in length, despite the two men sharing the same listed height.
  • Age: M'billi is 31 to Álvarez's 35.

Both men are orthodox, so this is not a clash of contrasting styles on paper. It is a younger, longer, heavier-handed challenger set against experience and ring-craft.

What to watch

The interesting battle is stylistic. M'billi will want to use that reach and his finishing pressure on an opponent who has rarely been overwhelmed. Álvarez, increasingly a points fighter, will look to drag a younger, longer man into the deep rounds where M'billi has spent comparatively little time. That trade, finishing power and length against accumulated experience, is the fight.
